doc.id: Potkay et al. - 2021 - Coupled whole-tree optimality and xylem hydraulics explain dynamic biomass partitioning.pdf
doc.title: Coupled whole-tree optimality and xylem hydraulics explain dynamic biomass partitioning

model_card.name: Potkay2021_Supplement_S3_XylemHydraulics
task: Steady-state transpiration from hydraulic supply + vapor diffusion demand

assumptions.text: Steady-state hydraulics: transpiration is represented by a hydraulic network linking soil layers → root collar (ΨRc) → stem (ΨS) → leaf (ΨL), with embolism reducing conductance via vulnerability curves.

evidence.snippet: Section S.3 introduces xylem potentials (ΨRc, ΨS, ΨL) and layer contributions Ei to leaf-area specific transpiration E via hydraulic resistances.

assumptions.text: Embolism effects are handled with an integral Kirchhoff transform of Weibull vulnerability curves to obtain an effective embolism factor f(·) along each segment (root/stem/leaf).

evidence.snippet: Text defines embolism factor via integral Kirchhoff transform and Weibull functions with parameters bX, cX.

assumptions.text: Transpiration is also expressed by a vapor diffusion relationship using Gw, saturation vapor pressure (ea,sat), and RH; the model solves for consistent E and water potentials.

evidence.snippet: Text defines transpiration from diffusional conductance Gw, ea,sat, and RH (Eq. S.3.10).

equations.explanation: Layer-i contribution to leaf-area-specific transpiration from soil layer i to root collar through the layer’s total root hydraulic resistance.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.1): aL Ei = (Ψsoil,i − ΨRc − ϖρgz_i) / RR,i (layout inferred from extracted line-wrapping).

equations.selection_reason: Fraction layout was partially lost in text extraction; numerator/denominator reconstructed from surrounding notation.

equations.explanation: Total root resistance to the i-th soil layer as radial resistance in layer i plus cumulative vertical resistances from surface to depth i.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.2): RR,i = RR,H,i + Σ_{j=1..i} RR,V,j.

equations.explanation: Radial root resistance parameterized by a proportionality constant and the conductive root carbon pool, reduced by embolism factor fR,i.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.3): RR,H,i expressed via βR,H, fR,i, and CR,H,i (exact formatting inferred).

equations.selection_reason: Exact subscripts/spacing depend on PDF math layout.

equations.explanation: Vertical (inter-layer) root resistance parameterized by conductive root carbon in vertical pathway and layer thickness.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.5): RR,V,i defined from βR,V, Δz_i, CR,V,i (exact placement of Δz_i^2 inferred).

equations.selection_reason: Exponent on Δz_i and exact arrangement inferred from extracted math text.

equations.explanation: Effective root embolism factor as an average vulnerability along the potential drop (integral Kirchhoff transform form).

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.4): fR,i defined via integral Kirchhoff transform; vulnerability curve VCR(Ψ) with parameters bR, cR.

equations.selection_reason: Piecewise structure reconstructed; exact equality-case line may differ in notation.

equations.explanation: Stem segment flow relation between root collar and stem potential including gravitational term.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.6): aL E = fS kS(ΨRc − ΨS − ϖρgH) (math layout partially wrapped).

equations.selection_reason: Term ordering inferred from extracted line breaks.

equations.explanation: Effective stem embolism factor from vulnerability curve VCS(Ψ) (Weibull parameters bS, cS).

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.7): fS defined via integral Kirchhoff transform using VCS(Ψ) with parameters bS, cS.

equations.selection_reason: Exact equality-case symbol may be VC_S(ΨRc) or VC_S(ΨS); not fully verifiable from current extracted text.

equations.explanation: Leaf segment flow relation between stem potential and leaf potential.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.8): E = fL kL(ΨS − ΨL).

equations.explanation: Effective leaf embolism factor from vulnerability curve VCL(Ψ) (Weibull parameters bL, cL).

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.9): fL defined via integral Kirchhoff transform using VCL(Ψ) with parameters bL, cL.

equations.selection_reason: Exact equality-case may reference VC_L(ΨS) or VC_L(ΨL); not fully verifiable here.

equations.explanation: Transpiration from vapor diffusion: leaf conductance to water vapor, saturation vapor pressure, and relative humidity.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.10): E defined by diffusional conductance Gw, ea,sat, and RH.

equations.explanation: Definition relating transpiration to canopy conductance integrated over leaf water potential from current ΨL to the E=0 reference ΨL,0.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.25): E = ∫ k_c dΨ between ΨL and ΨL,0.

equations.explanation: Leaf water potential at E=0 given by root-collar reference potential minus gravitational drop to canopy height.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.26): ΨL,0 = ΨRc,0 − ϖρgH.

equations.explanation: Root-collar water potential at E=0 obtained as a resistance-weighted average of soil layer potentials including gravitational term.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.27): ΨRc,0 expressed with Σ (Ψsoil,i − ϖρgz_i)/RR,0,i over Σ 1/RR,0,i (fraction layout reconstructed).

equations.selection_reason: Equation fraction bars/parentheses were partially lost in extracted math layout.

equations.explanation: Maximum canopy conductance in the E→0 reference state (using fL,0, fS,0, and an intermediate term \mathbb{c}_0 defined in S.3.19h / S.3.29 context).

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.32): kc,max = 1 / [ aL/(fL,0 kL) + 1/(c0 + fS,0 kS) ] (structure inferred).

equations.selection_reason: Term \mathbb{c}_0 appears in later derivative expressions; its exact definition requires the full Eq. S.3.19h context.

equations.explanation: Leaf embolism factor at E=0 evaluated at the E=0 leaf potential.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.33): fL,0 = VCL(ΨRc,0 − ϖρgH).

equations.explanation: Stem embolism factor at E=0 computed as average vulnerability across the gravitational drop from root collar to canopy.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.34): fS,0 defined by an integral of VCS(Ψ) over a Ψ-interval of size ϖρgH.

equations.selection_reason: Exact integral limits verified qualitatively; precise notation may vary.

equations.explanation: Derived expression for canopy conductance as an exponential of an integral involving a marginal value function λ(Ψ) and A_{n,max}.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.35d): kc = kc,max exp[ An,max ∫ λ dΨ ] (as extracted; exact symbols λ(Ψ) and limits ΨL→ΨL,0).

equations.selection_reason: The full derivation chain (S.3.35a–c) includes optimality conditions; exact interpretive mapping depends on prior definitions.

equations.explanation: Maximum transpiration defined by integrating canopy conductance from a critical leaf potential to the E=0 reference.

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.42): Emax = ∫ kc dΨ between ΨL,crit and ΨL,0.

equations.latex: RAW_BLOCKS_UNICODE: (S.3.49) 𝕘 = 𝐴𝑛 / (1+𝜆crit kc,max A_{n,max}^2(ΨL−ΨL,crit)) ; (S.3.50–S.3.55) define 𝕙, 𝕚 and assemble dE/dε expressions.
equations.explanation: Helper terms (𝕘,𝕙,𝕚) and assembled derivative expressions used to compute sensitivities of E to traits ε; kept as raw due to unavailable full math layout in current extraction.

evidence.snippet: Equations (S.3.49)–(S.3.55) appear as a block defining 𝕘, 𝕙, 𝕚 and the combined derivative formula for dE/dε.

equations.selection_reason: Raw blocks were observed earlier in-session, but exact LaTeX reconstruction for all terms is not guaranteed without re-reading the PDF math objects.

variables.name: leaf area
variables.definition: Leaf area used to convert total flow to leaf-area-specific transpiration and to scale stem flow equation.

evidence.snippet: Text: “where aL is the leaf area [m2] …”

variables.name: leaf-area-specific transpiration
variables.definition: Total transpiration expressed per unit leaf area.

evidence.snippet: Text introduces E as leaf-area specific transpiration; later Eq. (S.3.10) provides a vapor-diffusion form.

variables.name: layer contribution to transpiration
variables.definition: Contribution from soil layer i to total leaf-area-specific transpiration.

evidence.snippet: Text: “each layer contributes its portion of transpiration, Ei, to the leaf-area specific transpiration, E.”

variables.name: soil water potential (layer i)
variables.definition: Water potential in soil layer i driving root water uptake.

evidence.snippet: Text references Ψsoil,i [MPa] in the ith soil layer in the root flow equation context.

variables.name: root collar water potential
variables.definition: Xylem water potential at the root collar (junction of roots and stem).

evidence.snippet: Section S.3 introduces ΨRc as a xylem potential.

variables.name: stem xylem water potential
variables.definition: Xylem water potential in the stem segment.

evidence.snippet: Section S.3 introduces ΨS as a xylem potential.

variables.name: leaf water potential
variables.definition: Xylem/leaf water potential at the leaf end of the hydraulic pathway.

evidence.snippet: Section S.3 introduces ΨL as a xylem potential.

variables.name: root hydraulic resistance to layer i
variables.definition: Total hydraulic resistance from root collar to soil layer i.

evidence.snippet: Text: “RR,i is the hydraulic resistance … [MPa · s · (mol H2O)-1] …”

variables.name: maximum stem conductance
variables.definition: Maximum stem hydraulic conductance, reduced by embolism factor fS.

evidence.snippet: Text: “kS is the maximum stem conductance [mol H2O · s-1 · MPa-1] … and fS is a …”

variables.name: leaf area-specific maximum leaf conductance
variables.definition: Leaf hydraulic conductance per leaf area, reduced by embolism factor fL.

evidence.snippet: Text: “leaf area-specific maximum leaf conductance, kL [mol H2O · m-2 · s-1 · MPa-1] … and fL is a …”

variables.name: diffusional conductance to water vapor
variables.definition: Leaf-to-air diffusional conductance used in vapor diffusion expression for transpiration.

evidence.snippet: Text: “diffusional conductance … Gw [mol …]” in Eq. (S.3.10) context.

variables.name: canopy conductance (hydraulic)
variables.definition: Defined as kc = -dE/dΨL; used in integral representation of E and in k_c,max definition.

evidence.snippet: Text: “kc = -dE/dΨL is the canopy conductance [mol H2O · m-2 · s-1 · MPa-1] …”
Text defines βR,H with units [MPa · s · (mol H2O)-1 · mol C] describing proportionality for radial resistance.

parameters.name: βR,V
parameters.unit: MPa s (mol H2O)^-1 mol C m^-2

evidence.snippet: Text defines βR,V with units for vertical resistance proportionality (exact exponent terms depend on Δz_i usage).
Text: Weibull parameters bR [MPa] and cR [-] for effective root vulnerability curve VCR(Ψ).
Text: Weibull parameters bS [MPa] and cS [-] for stem vulnerability curve VCS(Ψ).
Text: Weibull parameters bL [MPa] and cL [-] for leaf vulnerability curve VCL(Ψ).

steps: Step 1: Compute root vertical and radial resistances per layer (RR,V,i and RR,H,i) from conductive root carbon pools and proportionality constants; combine to RR,i using Eq. (S.3.2).

Step 2: For a candidate root-collar potential ΨRc (or candidate E), compute effective embolism factors fR,i, fS, fL from vulnerability curves using the integral Kirchhoff transform (Eqs. S.3.4, S.3.7, S.3.9).
Step 3: Compute each layer’s transpiration contribution Ei from the hydraulic driving gradient between Ψsoil,i and ΨRc through RR,i (Eq. S.3.1). Sum to obtain E = ΣEi.
Step 4: Enforce consistency across the aboveground pathway by relating E to ΨS via the stem equation (Eq. S.3.6) and to ΨL via the leaf equation (Eq. S.3.8).
Step 5: Enforce atmospheric demand using vapor diffusion relationship (Eq. S.3.10). Solve for the unknown(s) (commonly E and potentials) so that hydraulic supply and diffusion demand agree.
Step 6 (reference state): Compute E=0 reference potentials ΨRc,0 and ΨL,0 (Eqs. S.3.27–S.3.26) and the associated fL,0 and fS,0 (Eqs. S.3.33–S.3.34). Use these to compute kc,max (Eq. S.3.32).
Step 7 (optional sensitivities): Use the analytic derivative system (Eqs. S.3.11–S.3.62) to compute dE/dε for traits ε (e.g., D, aL, CR,H,i, CR,V,i) as required by the optimality/allocation module.
algorithm.pseudocode: Given inputs, define functions RR(i), fR(i,ΨRc), fS(ΨRc,ΨS), fL(ΨS,ΨL). Use a root-finding loop on E (or ΨRc) to satisfy: (i) E = ΣEi(ΨRc), (ii) aL E = fS kS(ΨRc-ΨS-ϖρgH), (iii) E = fL kL(ΨS-ΨL), (iv) E = Gw ea,sat (1-RH). Compute E=0 references and kc,max; optionally compute analytic sensitivities via supplied equations.

evidence.snippet: Section S.3 defines ΨRc, ΨS, ΨL, layer contributions Ei, and resistances; later gives diffusion-demand Eq. (S.3.10) and conductance definitions.

limitations.text: Several intermediate helper terms in the analytic sensitivity derivations (e.g., \mathbb{a},\mathbb{b},\mathbb{c},\mathbb{d},\mathbb{e}, and 𝕘/𝕙/𝕚 blocks) require exact PDF math layout to reliably reconstruct full LaTeX for all sub-equations S.3.11–S.3.62.

evidence.snippet: Later pages in S.3 contain long analytic derivative blocks; only partial reconstruction is provided here.

implementation.model_scope: Hydraulic submodel (roots + stem + leaf) and associated analytic sensitivities from Supplement Section S.3.
control_inputs: (implicit) stomatal regulation via Gw or via coupling in other sections (not fully specified in S.3)

io_contract.input_schema: Inputs: soil water potentials per layer, root carbon pools, vulnerability curve parameters, conductances (kS,kL), geometry (H,z_i,Δz_i), atmospheric demand variables (Gw, ea,sat, RH).
io_contract.output_schema: Outputs: transpiration E and layer shares Ei, xylem potentials (ΨRc,ΨS,ΨL), canopy conductances (kc,kc,max), E=0 reference potentials (ΨRc,0, ΨL,0), and optional Emax given ΨL,crit.

evidence.snippet: S.3 defines the hydraulic variables/potentials and links to atmospheric demand; derivative machinery suggests the submodel feeds optimality/allocation.
Edges follow directly from Eqs. (S.3.1–S.3.10) and the reference-state conductance definitions (S.3.25–S.3.34).

numerics.integration_method: Steady-state (algebraic + integrals over Ψ) with root-finding for consistency.
numerics.solver: Not specified in S.3; likely a numerical root-finder for coupled nonlinear equations in (E, ΨRc, ΨS, ΨL).
numerics.stability_notes: Nonlinearities due to vulnerability curves; piecewise cases when potential differences approach zero.

evidence.snippet: S.3 provides analytic derivatives suggesting Newton-like sensitivity-based solution may be intended, but solver details are not explicit.

initial_conditions.name: E=0 reference potentials
initial_conditions.value: Compute ΨRc,0 (Eq. S.3.27) and ΨL,0 (Eq. S.3.26) when E=0.

evidence.snippet: Eqs. (S.3.26–S.3.27) define the E=0 reference potentials.

boundary_conditions.name: Soil layer potentials
boundary_conditions.formulation: Ψsoil,i specified as input for each soil layer i.

evidence.snippet: Ψsoil,i appears as prescribed driver in the layer flux equation (S.3.1).

boundary_conditions.name: Atmospheric demand
boundary_conditions.formulation: E must satisfy E = Gw ea,sat (1-RH) (Eq. S.3.10).

evidence.snippet: Eq. (S.3.10) provides atmospheric diffusion demand for transpiration.

steps: Ensure consistent sign convention for water potentials (MPa) and for gravitational term (ϖρgz, ϖρgH).
Handle the Ψ1=Ψ2 limit cases in embolism-factor definitions (piecewise forms for fR,fS,fL).
If using numerics, ensure integration limits for Kirchhoff transforms are ordered correctly with sign.
unit_conversions: Potential conversion: if ρgH is computed in Pa, ϖ likely converts Pa→MPa (exact definition not explicitly extracted in this chunk).

evidence.snippet: S.3 includes explicit MPa and conductance units; gravitational conversion factor ϖ is used but not explicitly defined in the extracted text.
S.3 defines parameter units for β and Weibull curve parameters; estimation method is not described in this chunk.

modules.responsibility: Implement S.3 xylem hydraulics: compute resistances, embolism factors, solve E and potentials, compute kc and reference-state values; optionally compute analytic sensitivities.
